title: GPT-3: The In-Context Learning Era
concept: GPT-3 scaled to 175 billion parameters, demonstrating that massive scale unlocked in-context few-shot learning directly from prompts.

exercises.prompt: What major change led to the discovery of GPT-3's in-context learning abilities?

options.text: Scaling up the size of the model to 175 billion parameters.
Using only images instead of text for training.
Limiting the model to only write short dictionary definitions.

exercises.explain: GPT-3 showed that simply making a model much larger (scaling parameters) allowed it to perform tasks it wasn't specifically trained on, just by reading prompts.

cards.title: No Retraining Required!
cards.body: Before GPT-3, if you wanted an AI to write poems or translate code, you had to carefully retrain its neural network for that specific job. GPT-3 changed everything: you could just show it a few examples of what you wanted, and it figured it out instantly!

cards.title: What is In-Context Learning?
cards.body: In-context learning is the ability of a language model to understand a new task and how to perform it just by reading a few examples provided directly inside the prompt, without changing any of its permanent settings.

cards.title: The Instant Scholar
cards.body: Imagine a world-class translator who doesn't need to go back to school to learn a new style of slang. Instead, you just show them three quick examples of the slang in a text message, and they immediately understand the pattern and continue translating perfectly. GPT-3 does the exact same thing within its chat window!

cards.title: Few-Shot Prompting in Action
cards.body:
If you prompt GPT-3 with:
'Happy -> Good'
'Sad -> Bad'
'Excited -> ...'

GPT-3 immediately understands the pattern and outputs 'Good' or 'Positive' because it reads and learns from the context you just typed.

cards.title: Does GPT-3 Permanent-Learn?
cards.body: Some people think that when GPT-3 learns from your prompt, it permanently updates its system memory. In reality, it only 'remembers' the examples during that specific conversation. Once the chat is closed, it resets back to its default state.

cards.title: Traditional Tuning vs. Prompt Learning
cards.body: See how the way we train and use AI changed with the arrival of GPT-3's scale.

left.text: Required training the model on new data files to change its internal weights for every new task.

right.text: Just type a few examples into the chat window and the model understands the task instantly.

exercises.prompt: Approximately how many parameters did GPT-3 have to unlock its in-context learning capabilities?
exercises.explain: GPT-3 was scaled up to an unprecedented 175 billion parameters, which unlocked its powerful pattern-matching abilities.

exercises.prompt: True or False: GPT-3 can learn to perform a new task without the developers updating its underlying software code.
exercises.explain: In-context learning allows GPT-3 to perform brand new tasks simply by reading examples in the prompt, without any system code updates.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe where GPT-3 looks for examples to learn a new task.
exercises.sentence: GPT-3 learns how to perform a new task using examples provided directly in the prompt.
exercises.explain: In-context learning works because the model analyzes the patterns and examples we type directly into the prompt.

exercises.prompt: Which of these responses demonstrates an AI performing 'in-context learning' from a prompt?

options.text: Prompt: 'Write a long story about space.' -> Response: 'Once upon a time in a galaxy...'
Prompt: 'Apple: Fruit, Carrot: Vegetable, Potato: ...' -> Response: 'Vegetable'
Prompt: 'Hello' -> Response: 'I do not understand this pattern.'

exercises.explain: Response B shows a model utilizing in-context learning by completing the pattern given in the prompt, whereas A is just simple text completion and C is an error message.

exercises.prompt: Why was GPT-3's scale of 175 billion parameters such a massive breakthrough for in-context learning?

options.text: It had to be retrained on millions of custom examples for every new task.
Its massive size unlocked the ability to learn new tasks directly from examples in the prompt.
It shrank in size to run faster on mobile phone chips.
It completely stopped using neural networks and switched to traditional rules.

exercises.explain: By scaling to 175 billion parameters, GPT-3 became so good at spotting patterns that it could learn how to perform new tasks on the fly just by looking at a few examples in the prompt, without modifying its underlying brain.
